title: Request Router — Command Dispatch
description: Create a RequestRouter class that maps command strings to handler functions. Support registering handlers with a @router.route('command') decorator pattern. Include a dispatch(request) method that splits the request into a command and arguments, looks up the handler, and calls it. If no handler is found, raise a ValueError. The dispatch method should accept a string like 'get key1' and call the registered 'get' handler with ['key1'] as arguments.
acceptance_criteria: RequestRouter stores handlers in an internal dict
route(command) works as a decorator that registers the handler function
The decorator returns the original function unchanged
dispatch(request) splits the request string into command and arguments
dispatch calls the correct handler with a list of argument strings
dispatch raises ValueError for unregistered commands
hints: The route() method should return a decorator: def decorator(func): self._routes[command] = func; return func
In dispatch(), use request.split() to get parts. parts[0] is the command, parts[1:] are args.
If parts[0] not in self._routes, raise ValueError(f'Unknown command: {parts[0]}')
The handler receives args as a list of strings, even if empty: handler(parts[1:])
